headline: Texas brings defensive momentum to face UCLA's offensive rebuild
subhead: Second-ranked Longhorns test their resilience against a Bruins squad seeking identity after a 23-point home loss.

xFactor.why: Texas held Ohio State to 15 points and will pressure UCLA's offense, which struggled to protect its quarterback against Michigan's pass rush.

keyMatchup: Texas defensive line versus UCLA's offensive line will determine whether the Bruins can establish any rhythm or whether Texas forces another uncomfortable outing from their quarterback.
body: Texas enters the Coliseum riding genuine defensive credentials after dismantling Ohio State's offense for most of the second half last week. The Longhorns' ability to generate pressure without blitzing excessively gives them a natural advantage against UCLA, whose offensive line allowed seven sacks in a 51-28 rout at home to Michigan. This is not a mismatch on paper, but it is a clash of momentum: Texas's defense is clicking while UCLA's offense has yet to stabilize.

UCLA's pathway to an upset runs through keeping the game lower-scoring and forcing Texas into one-dimensional football. The Bruins' defense showed competence in stretches against Michigan despite the lopsided score, and they'll need that unit to impose discipline early. However, UCLA's inability to mount drives in the second half against the Wolverines raises a red flag—if Texas gets out to a two-score lead, the Bruins may lack the offensive firepower to answer quickly.
